pattern: A subtree is perfect exactly when its two halves are perfect and equally deep, so the whole perfect-subtree census falls out of one bottom-up pass.
hints: A subtree can only be perfect if both of its children are themselves perfect and reach exactly the same depth below it.
Process the tree bottom-up: at each node, combine what you learned about its left and right children to decide if this node roots a perfect subtree, and if so, how big it is.
Collect every perfect subtree's size as you go, sort them from largest to smallest, and pick out the k-th one -- or report that it doesn't exist.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, the whole tree is perfect
statement example, k larger than the number of perfect subtrees
a single node: it is trivially its own perfect subtree
one lopsided child breaks perfection at the root but not below
a full depth-4 perfect tree: several perfect subtrees of different sizes coexist
left and right subtrees are each perfect but at different depths, so the root itself is not

stress.description: randomly grown binary trees in LeetCode level-order wire form, with random k
